verdict.label: Probably skip
verdict.headline: No reliable window
verdict.reason: Surfline and Surf-Forecast agree that the useful windows are poor.

conversationalSummary.text: Tomorrow is probably one to skip. Molhe Leste at After work (16:30 start) looks like the least-bad option, but not really something worth chasing. The lack of push looks like the main issue and could easily make it messy. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam first, but unless it looks surprisingly clean, I'd save the drive.

best.bestWind: ideal N/NNE/NE, usable NNW/ENE/E, clean <=25 km/h

photo.alt: Photo of Molhe Leste surf spot

wetsuit.reason: 4/3 remains the safer default for Peniche unless water is warm and wind is light.

best.explanation: Molhe Leste looks like a small window for Too soft, Low confidence: 0-0.3 m, energy not captured, 8 s period, lully 0/100, and wind is not fully clear.  A jetty-shaped beach break with punchy rights and lefts; this is best for sheltered power when Supertubos has too much size; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak for Supertubos / Molhe Leste.  Watch: Surfline consistency is lully; spot surf looks too small; 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak for Supertubos / Molhe Leste; Molhe Leste looks below your bodyboard fun size.
best.shortExplanation: Molhe Leste is worth a quick check for after work (16:30 start), but it is not a guaranteed session.  It looks too soft to make the drive worthwhile.  The wind should be manageable, but not a big advantage.  The tide is leaning more serious than easy here.
warnings: 6-9s at high tide is too flat and weak for Supertubos / Molhe Leste
Molhe Leste looks below your bodyboard fun size
